brief_justification: Correct answer (No) with valid causal reasoning about collider bias; Multi-level analysis tracing d-separation rules, collider activation, and spurious correlation mechanics; Some self-monitoring via suppress/amplify framing but no explicit bias-checking mid-reasoning; Verification through the d-separation table contrasting conditioned vs unconditioned states; Acknowledges ground truth has zero causal effect but doesn't explicitly quantify confidence; Systematically contrasts conditioning vs not conditioning with clear table showing why alternative is wrong; Full derivation from causal graph to d-separation to collider bias to conclusion, fully reproducible
